page: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a Cloud GPU family server designed for workloads requiring high-density graphics processing and substantial system memory. The server features 48 physical x86_64 cores powered by an Intel Core Processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 496.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 10.33 GB of RAM per core.

The primary hardware highlight is the inclusion of eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 128 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the instance is bundled with a 1500 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-speed local data access. Additionally, the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In terms of performance, benchmark data indicates a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1781.09 falls within the average category. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 73907.77 is rated as strong, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested servers. This multi-core strength is supported by CPU flags such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of GPU resources and system memory relative to its shared CPU allocation. This makes it a specialized option for workloads that prioritize parallel GPU processing over dedicated single-core CPU performance. Recommended workloads include high-density virtual desktop infrastructure, video streaming, Android cloud gaming, and parallelized GPU-accelerated computing tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with 48 physical x86_64 Intel cores running at 2.0 GHz on a shared allocation. It features 496.0 GB of system memory and is bundled with eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing 128 GB of total VRAM, alongside a 1500 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1781.09, but strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 73907.77, placing it in the top 25 percent. This hardware profile offers high resource density for GPU-heavy tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as high-density virtual desktop infrastructure, video streaming, cloud gaming, and parallel GPU-accelerated computing.
